import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ line: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  mut current = ""
  let n = string.len(line)
  mut i = 0
  while i < n
    mut byte = 0
    match string.byte_at(line, i)
    when Some(b)
      byte = b
    when None
      ()
    end
    if byte == 32 or byte == 9 or byte == 13 or byte == 10
      if not string.is_empty(current)
        match string.parse_int(current)
        when Some(v)
          result = list.push(result, v)
        when None
          ()
        end
        current = ""
      end
    else
      current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(byte)}"
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if not string.is_empty(current)
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn main() -> Unit
  mut line1 = ""
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    line1 = s
  when None
    ()
  end
  mut line2 = ""
  match io.read_line()
  when Some(s)
    line2 = s
  when None
    ()
  end
  let nums = parse_ints(line1)
  mut t = 0
  match string.parse_int(string.trim(line2))
  when Some(v)
    t = v
  when None
    ()
  end
  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  for x in nums
    if x > t
      if first
        out = "#{x}"
        first = false
      else
        out = "#{out} #{x}"
      end
    end
  end
  println(out)
end
